STAT508_DAA7

Ada Lazuli

Tuesday, March 17, 2026

Front Matter

#Delete all objects from Environment - Use with caution
remove(list = ls()) 

#Load Libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(gridExtra)
library(splines)
library(mgcv)
library(reactable) 

#Read in Boston Housing Data
biolum <- read.csv("../../sample_data/L07_Biolum.csv")

Problem 1 - Initial Exploration

Part a. - Create a scatterplot showing bioluminescence as a function of sample depth for the Season 2 data. Add a linear trend line to the plot. Comment on what you have learned from the plot. As part of this, be sure to address the research question.

ggplot(biolum, aes(x = SampleDepth, y = Sources)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Level versus Sample Depth",
       x = "Sample Depth",
       y = "Sources")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

It appears that as the sample depth increases, the bioluminescence decreases. The scatter plot suggests that this function is nonlinear.

Part b. - Create a scatterplot showing bioluminescence as a function of sample depth, and facet on season. Comment on what you have learned from the plot. As part of this, be sure to address the adjusted research question.

ggplot(biolum, aes(x = SampleDepth, y = Sources)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Level versus Sample Depth",
       x = "Sample Depth",
       y = "Sources") +
  facet_wrap(~Season)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

This facet wrap shows that the season definitely impacts the bio-luminescence level.

Problem 2 - Polynomial Regression

Part a. - Build a linear regression model for predicting bioluminescence using sample depth. Name the result model0 and display a summary of the model.

model0 <- lm(Sources ~ SampleDepth, data = biolum)
summary(model0)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Sources ~ SampleDepth, data = biolum)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -13.730  -5.618  -1.777   3.570  56.324 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 26.9090190  0.6473273   41.57   <2e-16 ***
## SampleDepth -0.0077187  0.0002757  -28.00   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 8.305 on 787 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.499,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.4983 
## F-statistic: 783.7 on 1 and 787 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Part b. - Fit a degree 3 polynomial regression model (i.e., a cubic regression model) for predicting bioluminescence using sample depth. Name the result model1 and display a summary of the model.

model1 <- lm(Sources ~ SampleDepth + I(SampleDepth^2) + I(SampleDepth^3), data = biolum)
summary(model1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Sources ~ SampleDepth + I(SampleDepth^2) + I(SampleDepth^3), 
##     data = biolum)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -17.275  -4.497  -0.874   1.484  52.794 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       3.304e+01  2.248e+00  14.699  < 2e-16 ***
## SampleDepth      -1.166e-02  3.548e-03  -3.286  0.00106 ** 
## I(SampleDepth^2) -1.054e-06  1.608e-06  -0.655  0.51239    
## I(SampleDepth^3)  4.670e-10  2.163e-10   2.159  0.03114 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 7.792 on 785 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.5601, Adjusted R-squared:  0.5584 
## F-statistic: 333.1 on 3 and 785 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Part c. - Find the AIC for model0 and model1. (Be sure the AIC values are displayed in your Markdown document.) Which model is preferred based on the AIC values?

AIC(model0)
## [1] 5583.537
AIC(model1)
## [1] 5484.898

Based on the AIC values, model1 is preferred due to the lower AIC value of 5484.989 compared to 5583.537.

Problem 3 - Regression Splines

Part a. - Using 4 equally spaced knots placed at the 20th, 40th, 60th, and 80th percentiles of SampleDepth, fit a cubic regression spline. Name the result model2 and display a summary of the model.

knots <- quantile(x = biolum$SampleDepth,
                  probs = c(0.2, .4, .6, .8))
model2 <- lm(Sources ~ bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots), data = biolum)
summary(model2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Sources ~ bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots), 
##     data = biolum)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -20.179  -3.007  -0.252   1.629  50.131 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                 Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                                       38.213      1.957  19.521
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)1  -23.214      3.425  -6.778
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)2  -18.205      2.126  -8.564
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)3  -18.148      2.604  -6.969
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)4  -40.015      2.235 -17.906
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)5  -32.577      3.254 -10.010
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)6  -42.314      3.632 -11.649
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)7  -35.567      3.848  -9.243
##                                                 Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                      < 2e-16 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)1 2.41e-11 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)2  < 2e-16 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)3 6.78e-12 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)4  < 2e-16 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)5  < 2e-16 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)6  < 2e-16 ***
## bs(x = SampleDepth, degree = 3, knots = knots)7  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 7.309 on 781 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.6149, Adjusted R-squared:  0.6114 
## F-statistic: 178.1 on 7 and 781 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Part b. - Find the AIC for model2. Based on AIC, would the cubic spline model be preferred to model0 or model1? Explain.

AIC(model2)
## [1] 5387.892

Based on the AIC values, model2 is preferred due the lower AIC value of 5387.892 compared to AIC values of 5583.537 and 5484.898 for models model0 and model1.

Problem 4 - Generalized Additive Models

Part a. - Fit a GAM for predicting bioluminescence using a smooth term for sample depth and a linear term for season. Since Season is an indicator, treat it as a factor. Name the result model3 and display a summary of the model. Comment on the effective degrees of freedom of the smooth terms.

biolum <- biolum %>% mutate(SeasonFactor = as.factor(Season))
model3 <- gam(Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + SeasonFactor, data = biolum)
summary(model3)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + SeasonFactor
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     7.2493     0.3613   20.07   <2e-16 ***
## SeasonFactor2   6.1631     0.4826   12.77   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                  edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## s(SampleDepth) 8.829  8.991 184.6  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.692   Deviance explained = 69.5%
## GCV = 42.991  Scale est. = 42.401    n = 789

Based on the summary, the SampleDepth has 8.829 effect degress of freedom and implies the feature is

Part b. - Plot the contributions of each feature.

plot(model3, all.terms = TRUE)

Part c. - Based on AIC values, is model3 preferred over the earlier models (models 0, 1, and 2)? Explain.

AIC(model3)
## [1] 5208.362

Based on the AIC values, model3 has the lowest value of 5208.362 and is therefore assumed to be the best model, pending further analysis and validation set error evaluation.

Part d. - Suppose that we wish to build upon the model constructed in Problem 4 Part a. that contains a smooth term for sample depth and a linear term for season by adding the relative depth variable to the model. Should you use a smooth term for relative depth, a linear term for relative depth, or should it not be included at all? Justify your choice based on the generalized cross validation error (GCV).

model3_smooth <- gam(Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + s(RelativeDepth) + SeasonFactor, data = biolum, cv = FALSE)
model3_linear <- gam(Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + RelativeDepth + SeasonFactor, data = biolum, cv = FALSE)
summary(model3_smooth)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + s(RelativeDepth) + SeasonFactor
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     7.9408     0.3452   23.00   <2e-16 ***
## SeasonFactor2   4.9587     0.4784   10.36   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## s(SampleDepth)   8.919  8.997 151.15  <2e-16 ***
## s(RelativeDepth) 8.753  8.981  20.45  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.749   Deviance explained = 75.5%
## GCV = 35.356  Scale est. = 34.475    n = 789
summary(model3_linear)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + RelativeDepth + SeasonFactor
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    9.7954541  0.6480069  15.116   <2e-16 ***
## RelativeDepth -0.0013968  0.0002969  -4.705    3e-06 ***
## SeasonFactor2  6.0485201  0.4768786  12.684   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                  edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## s(SampleDepth) 8.824  8.991 132.7  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =    0.7   Deviance explained = 70.4%
## GCV =  41.92  Scale est. = 41.292    n = 789

The GCV score for the model with the smooth function wrapper for relative depth is 35.36 while the model with the linear function for relative depth has a GCV of 41.92.

Based on the GCV scores, the model with the smooth function seems superior.

Problem 5 Estimating Test Set Error using GAMs

Part a. - Using a seed of 123, perform an 80/20 training/validation split.

set.seed(123)
trainInd <- sample(1:nrow(biolum), floor(0.8*nrow(biolum)))
set.seed(NULL)

Train <- biolum[trainInd, ]
Validation <- biolum[-trainInd, ]

Part b. - Calculate the validation set MSE using the GAM trained on smooth terms for sample depth and relative depth and a linear term for season.

model3_smooth <- gam(Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + s(RelativeDepth) + SeasonFactor, data = Train, cv = FALSE)
model3_smooth_pred <- predict(model3_smooth, newdata = Validation)
model3_smooth_MSE <- mean((Validation$Sources - model3_smooth_pred)^2)
summary(model3_smooth)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + s(RelativeDepth) + SeasonFactor
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     8.1140     0.3796  21.373   <2e-16 ***
## SeasonFactor2   5.1706     0.5285   9.784   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## s(SampleDepth)   8.894  8.995 121.23  <2e-16 ***
## s(RelativeDepth) 8.546  8.939  15.68  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.743   Deviance explained =   75%
## GCV = 35.301  Scale est. = 34.214    n = 631
model3_linear <- gam(Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + RelativeDepth + SeasonFactor, data = Train, cv = FALSE)
model3_linear_pred <- predict(model3_linear, newdata = Validation)
model3_linear_MSE <- mean((Validation$Sources - model3_linear_pred)^2)
summary(model3_linear)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## Sources ~ s(SampleDepth) + RelativeDepth + SeasonFactor
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   10.0593992  0.7224961   13.92  < 2e-16 ***
## RelativeDepth -0.0013386  0.0003257   -4.11 4.48e-05 ***
## SeasonFactor2  5.9785557  0.5294745   11.29  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                  edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## s(SampleDepth) 8.784  8.986 107.6  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.695   Deviance explained =   70%
## GCV = 41.395  Scale est. = 40.622    n = 631

Part c. - How does this value compare to the GCV value for this model? Explain.

df <- data.frame(Model = c("GAM Smooth Season", "GAM Linear Season"), GCV = c(35.301, 41.395), "MSE Validation" = c(round(model3_smooth_MSE, digits = 2), round(model3_linear_MSE, digits = 2)))
reactable(df, searchable = FALSE, compact = TRUE, striped = TRUE, defaultPageSize = 5, showPageSizeOptions = FALSE)

The GAM model with the smooth function for the RelativeDepth feature has a GCV value of 35.301 and MSE value of 36.63. These values are lower than the GAM model with the linear function for the RelativeDepth feature that has a GCV value of 41.395 and MSE of 44.53.

Based on the GCV and MSE against the validation set, tt appears that the GAM with the smooth function for RelativeDepth is superior. In hindsight, this makes sense as the effective degrees of freedom for that term is 8.546 and implies it is non-linear.
